title: No TLS/mTLS Support
description: BaseTransporter sends plain bytes without any encryption — all channels (TCP,UDS,IPC,WS,Subprocess) are unencrypted. Applications requiring secure network connections (A2A over internet, external gRPC, external webhooks) are fully exposed.
impact: All traffic is exposed — critical security breach in any non-isolated production environment
proposed_solution: Add TLSConfig to TransportConfig with cert/key/ca paths; modify TCPTransporter, WebSocketTransporter, SubprocessTransporter to use ssl_context in asyncio.open_connection()
evidence: transport/tcp.py:64-65: asyncio.open_connection() without ssl_context; transport/websocket.py:63-64: same issue; transport/transport_config.py: no TLS options; .obsolete/grpc.py had full TLS but is disabled

title: No Authentication/Authorization
description: No authentication layer — any process can connect to FabricService and send/receive messages unrestricted. No JWT, API keys, OAuth, or identity model.
impact: Any process on same machine or network can use fabric without restrictions — data leakage and unauthorized access
proposed_solution: Add AuthToken to FabricClient.send() carrying JWT/API key; FabricService validates token on receive(); add AuthConfig to AppConfig
evidence: wiring/assembler.py:68-81: FabricClient.send() lacks auth parameter; protocols/http_handler.py:37-55: HttpProtocolHandler lacks api_key/token; transport/base.py:124: ErrorType.PERMANENT references 'unauthorized' but no implementation exists

title: No Message-Level Encryption
description: PresentationPipeline only compresses (gzip/zstd) and does not encrypt — payload is exposed even with TLS because TLS protects transport only not storage/middleware.
impact: In multi-tenant or middleware scenarios, data may be visible to other applications or in logs
proposed_solution: Add optional EncryptionStage to PresentationPipeline using AES-GCM or libsodium via new IEncryptor protocol
evidence: presentation/pipeline.py:84-134: encode/decode does not encrypt, only serialize + compress; presentation/protocol.py: no IEncryptor protocol; presentation/codecs/: no encrypt/decrypt codec

title: No Secrets Management
description: No secure storage for TLS certs, API keys, shared_secrets, or tokens. Everything is read from plain YAML or passed as string in code. Production requires Vault/AKV/K8s Secrets.
impact: Keys and credentials will leak in production — all keys are in plaintext YAML or code
proposed_solution: Support env vars ($ENV_VAR) in ConfigLoader + optional Vault/HashiCorp/Azure Key Vault adapter as config source; encrypt sensitive fields in YAML
evidence: wiring/config/loader.py:67-139: load_config reads YAML only without os.getenv or vault integration; wiring/factories/protocol_provider.py:78: shared_secret=entry.endpoint — using endpoint as secret (security breach); transport/config/transport_config.py: no secrets config
notes: SEC-004 differs from SEC-008: SEC-004 focuses on storage and secret management, SEC-008 focuses on secret leakage in endpoint

title: No Input Validation / Content Sanitization
description: PresentationPipeline and protocol handlers perform no validation or sanitization on payloads. Data flows from network to application unchecked — Injection, XXE, Prototype Pollution attacks possible.
impact: Malicious payloads may reach handlers or core application without filtering — injection vulnerabilities and data integrity issues
proposed_solution: Add ValidationStage to PresentationPipeline with Pydantic schema validation or allowlist; sanitization for input/output in protocol handlers
evidence: presentation/pipeline.py:84-134: encode/decode does not inspect content; protocols/http_handler.py: no validation on body; protocols/graphql_handler.py: passes query directly to executor

title: No Security Audit Logging
description: No security audit trail (logs) — failed auth attempts, sensitive config changes, and unauthorized access are not logged. Breach detection and forensics are impossible. Violates SOC2/ISO27001/HIPAA.
impact: Cannot track security events — violates SOC2/ISO27001 and HIPAA requirements in regulated environments
proposed_solution: Add SecurityAuditLogger with events: AUTH_SUCCESS, AUTH_FAIL, CONFIG_CHANGE, ACCESS_DENIED; emit audit events in FabricClient and ConfigLoader
evidence: transport/base.py:129-134: build_retry_decision_hook unrelated to security; wiring/assembler.py: FabricClient has no audit; fastapi_router.py: no audit log for authentication

title: No Rate Limiting / DoS Protection
description: No protection against DoS attacks or sudden request bursts. Circuit breaker exists but focuses on service failures not attack protection.
impact: Resources (CPU, memory, connections) may be exhausted via rapid consecutive requests — service outage risk
proposed_solution: Add RateLimiter (token-bucket or sliding window) to ChannelPool and SessionDispatcher; configure max requests per session/channel
evidence: network/adapters/pool_adapter.py: no rate limiting; network/resilience/circuit_breaker.py: focuses on destination failure not attack protection; transport/factory.py: no rate limiting config

title: Insecure Credential Handling (endpoint as shared_secret)
description: In protocol_provider.py, entry.endpoint (URL/path field) is used as shared_secret for WebhookProtocolHandler. This is a security leak: endpoint is also used in logs and routing, exposing the secret.
impact: shared_secret appears in logs, debugging, and monitoring — immediate webhook HMAC breach risk
proposed_solution: Add separate shared_secret field to ProtocolEntryConfig in ConfigLoader; do not reuse endpoint for security purposes
evidence: wiring/factories/protocol_provider.py:78: shared_secret=entry.endpoint if entry.endpoint and len(entry.endpoint) > 10 else None — using endpoint (URL!) as secret; wiring/config/loader.py:52: ProtocolEntryConfig lacks shared_secret field

title: No Certificate / PKI Management Infrastructure
description: No certificate management infrastructure — no auto-generation, renewal, revocation, or CA chain validation. Even with TLS added, there is no system to manage keys and certificates.
impact: Certificates will expire or be compromised without centralized management — service interruption or security vulnerabilities
proposed_solution: Add CertificateManager with auto-renewal (Let's Encrypt/cert-manager), CA bundle management, cert rotation hooks; integrate with Vault/AKV for key storage
evidence: transport/config/transport_config.py: no cert/key/ca field; wiring/config/loader.py: no PKI config; .obsolete/grpc.py:27-37 (disabled) had root_certificates, private_key, certificate_chain but dead code
notes: TLS/mTLS requirements incomplete without PKI management
